evals.name: new-skill-no-evals
evals.prompt: I just finished writing a skill called log-analyzer that helps triage Kubernetes pod crash logs. I haven't written any tests. How do I evaluate it before shipping?
evals.expected_output: Designs both evals.json and trigger-evals.json, explains inline vs Claude Code split, runs output quality inline, provides trigger commands for Claude Code.
expectations: Proposes creating both evals.json (output quality) AND trigger-evals.json (triggering) — not just one
Explains that output quality can be run inline but triggering requires claude -p (Claude Code)
Writes at least 3 concrete output quality eval prompts specific to log analysis / Kubernetes crashes
Writes at least 6 trigger-evals.json queries (mix of should-trigger and should-not-trigger)
Should-not-trigger queries are near-misses, not obviously irrelevant (e.g. 'help me debug a failing CI job' not 'write a poem')
Asks user to review the eval cases before running

evals.name: baseline-before-change
evals.prompt: I want to rewrite the instructions section of my pdf skill to simplify the table extraction steps. Can you help?
evals.expected_output: Stops the user before any changes, establishes a baseline on both dimensions first, only then proceeds.
expectations: Explicitly says baseline must be established BEFORE making any changes
Checks whether evals.json exists — offers to create it if missing
Runs or describes running output quality evals on the current pdf skill first
Mentions triggering baseline and explains how to run it (even if user is in Claude.ai)
Records baseline scores before proceeding to the rewrite
Does NOT proceed with the rewrite before the baseline step is complete

evals.name: run-existing-evals
evals.expected_output: Reads the data-cleaner skill, simulates executing the eval inline, grades against the three expectations with evidence.
expectations: Acknowledges it will read the data-cleaner skill before running (or notes it needs access to it)
Simulates executing the eval prompt following the skill's instructions
Grades each of the three expectations individually with PASS/FAIL and cited evidence
Produces a summary with overall pass rate
Flags the inline grading caveat (self-grading bias) and suggests Claude Code for final validation
Does NOT just describe what the skill would do — actually traces through the execution

evals.name: triggering-diagnosis
evals.prompt: My skill called commit-message-writer never loads when developers ask things like 'help me write a good commit message for this change' or 'what should I put in the commit description?'. The current description is: 'Formats git commit messages following conventional commits spec.'
evals.expected_output: Identifies why the description misses those queries, suggests specific phrase additions, proposes a revised description, explains run_loop.py for Claude Code.
expectations: Identifies that 'write a commit message' and 'commit description' are missing from the current description
Explains WHY the description misses: it describes the format spec, not the user's intent
Suggests specific phrases to add (e.g. 'write a commit message', 'what to put in commit description', 'summarize my changes for a commit')
Produces a concrete revised description draft — not just advice
Mentions run_loop.py for automated optimization if in Claude Code
Does NOT suggest fixing the instructions body — correctly identifies this as a description problem
